page: Microsoft Azure L16aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine from the Laosv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It features a 3.7 GHz clock speed, 32 MB of L3 cache, and is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is bundled with 11,519 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing is a notable strength, ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core execution, including Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. Memory performance shows a distinct tradeoff: while small-block cached read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth are average, large-block uncached read bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Large-model LLM inference is poor, though text generation with smaller 135M models is strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L16aos_v4 offers a high density of local storage and memory relative to its compute allocation, making it highly resource-dense without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. This profile is well-suited for storage-heavy and database workloads, such as relational databases, caching layers, and text processing pipelines. The lack of a GPU and the weak uncached memory read bandwidth make it less suitable for large-scale deep learning or memory-bound high-performance computing, but it remains highly effective for general-purpose enterprise applications, database operations, and moderate-scale web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure L16aos_v4 is a storage-focused virtual machine from the Laosv4 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 vCPUs (8 physical cores), 128.0 GB of RAM, and a substantial 11,519 GB of local storage. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU performance and exceptional text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database execution. However, a key tradeoff is its weak large-block uncached memory read bandwidth. Lacking a GPU, this instance is not designed for heavy AI training, but its high storage capacity and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio make it highly cost-efficient for database hosting, text processing, and storage-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16aos_v4 is a Laosv4-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 11,519 GB of local storage, optimized for database and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16aos_v4 is a storage-optimized VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 11,519 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing for demanding database and text-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 11,519 GB of local storage
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier text processing performance
Tradeoff of weaker large-block uncached memory read bandwidth
Best suited for database hosting, text processing, and storage-heavy workloads
